chemicals.concentration_notes: or potency: In small, controlled amounts, salt is an essential nutrient required for a cat's body to properly regulate hydration and electrolytes4. Toxicity is heavily dependent on water consumption; it occurs when a cat ingests an excessive amount of salt without having continuous access to fresh drinking water to balance it out89. While the provided sources note that the acute oral lethal dose o
chemicals.description: in cats: Ingesting excessive salt leads to a condition known as salt toxicosis, hypernatremia, or sodium ion poisoning1. As the concentration of sodium in the blood serum increases, it creates a powerful osmotic gradient that rapidly draws water out of the body's cells (the intracellular and interstitial compartments) and into the extracellular fluid23. This causes severe systemic dehydration and
chemicals.name: Sodium Chloride (Sodium Ion)

description: Salt (sodium chloride) is toxic to cats at elevated doses. Cats have a very low sodium tolerance compared to humans, and their kidneys are highly efficient at regulating sodium under normal dietary conditions. Excessive sodium intake overwhelms this system, causing severe neurological and cardiovascular effects.
emergencyNote: If a cat ingests a toxic amount of salt
name: Salt
safetyNotes: Toxic dose: approximately 4g/kg body weight is lethal; smaller amounts cause significant symptoms; even salty snacks fed regularly cause cumulative harm
Mechanism: high serum sodium causes cell dehydration and cerebral oedema; severe disruption of neurological and cardiac function
Symptoms: excessive thirst, frequent urination, vomiting, diarrhea, weakness, tremors, seizures; severe cases cause kidney failure and death
Salty human foods (chips, cured meats, soy sauce) are the most common accidental exposure route
If your cat ingests a large amount of salt or salty food, contact your vet immediately

symptoms.name: If a cat ingests or is exposed to excessive amounts of salt without adequate access to fresh water
Severe Gastrointestinal Upset
symptoms.notes: The influx of excessive salt causes direct osmotic irritation and inflammation of the gastrointestinal mucosa, triggering vomiting and diarrhea
symptoms.onset: Within minutes to a few hours of ingestion

symptoms.name: Excessive Thirst and Urination
symptoms.notes: The extreme thirst is the cat's biological attempt to restore osmotic balance4. The excess sodium circulating in the blood draws fluid out of the body's cells, leading to severe cellular dehydration, which the body frantically tries to correct by seeking water

symptoms.name: Neurological Deficits and Brain Damage
symptoms.notes: The brain is uniquely vulnerable to rapid hypernatremia. As the sodium pulls water out of the brain's cells, it causes cerebral dehydration and physical cell shrinkage89. As the brain mass shrinks, it can pull away from the skull, potentially tearing delicate meningeal blood vessels and causing fata
symptoms.onset: Progressive (develops within minutes to hours as the sodium alters cell osmolarity)

symptoms.name: Cardiovascular and Respiratory Distress
symptoms.notes: These symptoms are often accompanied by an elevated body temperature and are secondary to the massive fluid shifts, electrolyte derangements, and systemic shock the body is experiencing

symptoms.name: Acute Kidney Failure
symptoms.notes: The severe, rapid systemic dehydration caused by the sodium drawing fluid out of the cells can overwhelm and destroy the kidneys, leading to acute kidney failure and death
symptoms.onset: Progressive (hours to days as the toxicosis advances)

treatments.name: If a cat ingests a toxic amount of salt

treatments.description: of the procedure: If the cat arrives at the clinic already experiencing severe neurological distress, the veterinarian will administer seizure-control agents (such as diazepam) to stop the convulsions. If severe brain swelling (cerebral edema) is suspected, medications like mannitol, dexamethasone, or dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) may be administered to help reduce the pressure in the brain1. Any situ
treatments.name: Emergency Neurological Stabilization (Seizure Control)
treatments.notes: This takes absolute priority if the cat is actively having seizures or is in a coma, as the severe cellular dehydration caused by hypernatremia directly damages the brain and can cause intracranial hemorrhage

treatments.description: of the procedure: For mild cases, or if the cat was caught ingesting salt before severe clinical signs have started, the offending food or salt source is immediately removed. The cat is then offered fresh water, but strictly in small amounts at frequent intervals while being closely observed4.... Any situation-specific
treatments.name: Immediate Source Removal and Controlled Oral Hydration
treatments.notes: It is highly dangerous to let a salt-poisoned cat drink a massive amount of pure water all at once. A sudden influx of fresh water can cause fluid to rapidly build up in the brain, drastically worsening neurological signs and causing fatal brain swelling56. Pet owners are also advised never to induc

treatments.description: of the procedure: The cat is hospitalized, and blood serum sodium levels are closely monitored. The veterinarian administers specific IV fluids (sometimes slightly hypertonic fluids) to correct the cat's free water deficit, dilute the sodium in the blood, and restore electrolyte balance over a period of 2 to 3 days1.... Any situation-specific
treatments.name: Judicious Intravenous (IV) Fluid Therapy and Electrolyte Monitoring
treatments.notes: This is the most complex and critical part of treating salt toxicosis. To survive the extreme salt levels, the cat's brain cells produce "idiogenic osmoles" to maintain their volume. Therefore, the veterinarian must lower the serum sodium concentration very slowly, at a strictly controlled rate of n

treatments.description: of the procedure: The veterinarian may administer antacids and/or antibiotics to alleviate the severe gastrointestinal irritation and inflammation caused by the heavy influx of salt7. Any situation-specific
treatments.name: Symptomatic Gastrointestinal Support
treatments.notes: This is used as an adjunctive treatment to help manage the vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal distress that frequently accompany an acute salt poisoning episode
